description: Create a 360-degree rotation animation of a classic B-DNA double helix. Fetch the 1BNA structure from the RCSB PDB. Set up a publication-quality representation of the DNA (e.g., showing the sugar-phosphate backbone as a cartoon and the nucleotide bases as sticks). Color the two complementary strands (Chain A and Chain B) distinctly so the double helix structure is visually clear. Configure a 120-frame timeline and apply a 360-degree rotation around the Y-axis (e.g., using the `mroll` command). Export the animation frames as a series of individual PNG files to `/home/ga/PyMOL_Data/images/` (using a prefix like `frame_` so they output as `frame_0001.png`, etc.). Finally, extract the nucleotide sequence of Chain A and determine the total number of base pairs, writing a brief report to `/home/ga/PyMOL_Data/1bna_report.txt` containing the PDB ID, the Chain A sequence, and the base pair count.
